item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: *noot noot*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this soon-ish after reading another scp which was about human colonization ships equipped with anomalous machinery to wipe out non-human intelligence on a freshly colonized planet, and for some reason I thought this and that are collaborations and I was like "dang! The remains of naturally evolved civilization, which succumbed to the Earthling invaders!"
Then I checked comments and realized it'd chupakabra, ha-ha. (I didn't look at the dates of posting)

Anyway, this is a solid writing and I had quite a bunch of fun reading it. My attention was slipping a lot recently, but this one kept me reading and reading. This is def-ly a big + from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Potrix caprarum

Potrix = tippler, drinker
Caprarum = of goats

You utter, utter bastard

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel very thick but, how can 2636a be in the mural pictures if it was born inside Foundation containment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I support chupacabra Jesus in all their endeavors
Awesome job dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, +more if I could. Poor momma. Also poor kids. Here's hoping the Foundation doesn't totally oblooterate them when they show up again (and that they don't totally oblooterate the foundation).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is super late to be posting here, but I just had to say something.

Potrix caprarum sapiens. Correct Latin makes me happy. Also, translating that is how I figured out it was a chupacabra. What does that say about me?

And on top of the little Latin bonus, the article itself is compelling and well-written. This gets a huge +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*reading Addendum 3* Oh, well, phew, that was a close one. Looks like we're in the clear.

*reads Addendum 4* …Well gosh.

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I think this may be my new favorite article on the site. Chupacabra Moses may be the coolest thing I've ever heard, and the concept of us being the Pharaoh in that situation is terrifying. This is just weird, interesting, and well thought out. I love it.

Funny thing is, I didn't pick up on it being a chupacabra until I read the comments, and that just made it better. I was really into cryptozoology as a kid and that was my favorite one. Anyway, a well deserved +1, and I wish I could give it more than that. Keep being awesome, Djoric.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think they summoned their gods. I think they made them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took you long enough to finally post this idea :P Glad you did.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: Much better than what I posted. Too bad it won't be used anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty awesome. Always been mildly interested in cryptozoology and seeing the Chupacabra being used as skip fuel in a unique way is pretty nifty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mostly. She is one of the last, they were wiped out more by the natives and less by the Spanish, and yes. Their gods are not happy about things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I would like some clarification. I know I already posted here a couple times, and I apologize if I'm being spammy, but here goes:

+Spoilers
-Spoilers

Ok, so, upon rereading, I think I've completely figured this out: the main SCP is one of the last of her species. They're just as smart as us, but haven't been around for a long time (possible anomalous origin). When humans settled the New World, we almost wiped them out. European colonization and the following decimation of local life, human or otherwise, didn't help. However, they had a religion, and their gods are very much real. They made a temple and an idol (or multiples of both). Their gods have been summoned, and the SCP-1000-style genocide of humanity is pending.


Am I hot or cold?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Potrix caprarum. "Drinker of goats." You magnificent bastard.

There's tantalizingly more story to this than I could tease out on a first reading. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I guess "Chupassiah" sounds better than "ancient leprous Exploud doomsday cult".

I have no idea what the -2's are even remotely meant to be, but the story comes through really well. Good work on this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
removing moving waste heat

Is that "moving" supposed to be there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. She's not been having a good time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel sorry for the mother, but 10/10 would upvote again.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: Ah, yeah, that would do it. Venom glands are internal, edited that bit to make it a bit more clear.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: People and domesticated animals both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unidentified (or Uncategorized) Anomalous Property.

Will feex tonal issues.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad I got it right :D
(the breasts are actually supposed to be the venom glands, but I kinda messed it up) I was pretty much going for fennec, but I didn't use a reference, so I forgot just how HUGE fennec ears are, like twice the size of their heads, it's crazy.

Them being super light does make the whole getting BTFO by humans make sense, I just have a hard time visualizing weight with height, but now it fits better in my head.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: I also thought they were suffering a blood-borne disease, possibly picked up from eating people. Or did I read that wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tone's a tad off in places ("Deep Sleep", "Cut down by humans") but I like it overall, even if I am confused as to what a UAP is.

Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded: I always imagined chupacabras with an elongated head, so the picture works.

item.title: Re: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded:
Well shit, that's pretty much perfect. Personally, I always imagined the ears as a bit bigger, something like a fennec fox, and I never considered that it'd have breasts at all (No reason to, with their mouthparts. Babby chupes feed off kills their mother has made and already dissolved, as their venom glands mature.) but this is still super, super good.

As for the fights, note the weight - these things are super light. A solid strike with a club will cripple them, especially in the legs.

item.title: Made a quick drawing
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Is it supposed to look something like this? researcher for scale
(drawing solely from a description is difficult).
Anyway, I greatly enjoyed this article, at first I was wondering where you were going with this, but Mariacabra is an awesome conclusion.

also how were these 3,4M!!?! creatures defeated by the tiny tiny humans, that's like going against the Na'vi without mechs and guns

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show Revvalayatuns
- Hide Revvalayatuns

2636a is a red herring. The actual messiah is her older sister. Momma Chupe believed that her first daughter was killed during the Foundation's containment attempt, and so attempted to make another. Without the influence of 2636-2, second daughter wasn't able to make the cut.

The older sister is not dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) That is not a photo.

2) It does not resemble the entity in question.

3) That is probably the most commonplace image of a chupacabra to exist on the internet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2636's native form of communication, a series of song-like vocalizations is not yet understood.

I think you accidentally a comma.

Overall I don't really understand it well but I feel terribly sorry for the mother.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it is a drawing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First Bigfoot, now a chupacabra. What other modern mythical species have been tackled on so far?

And why not add a photo like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvote, as per Djoric-Reader Protocol III. However:

This central chamber of is built above a natural cave formation

That "of" is not supposed to be there, right? Am I missing something? :/

Ah, and this is such a clear shot out to NEG that I didn't even have to reach the comments to notice. I'm getting better at reading Djoric articles.

Also, EWU is still around! Yay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… fuck, how?

A very strong Not My Problem Field, both in and out of universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
..

I keep throwing money at this, and yet there is still no movies! What sorcery is that!? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not necessarily.

It would have the term "Leviathan" stamped all over that sucker, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somehow, I'm not a fan of this.
Perhaps because I didn't catch this being chupacabra (perhaps because the description "mammal-like reptile" doesn't invoke anything meaningful in my head) from the article itself ,and well, not being all that much into that particular myth.
Or that them having a civillization which was somehow purged by humans (of which there aren't many records anywhere if recent enough, and … fuck, how? if old enough.) and all the stuff which culminated in the Foundation terminating Ms. Chupa-Moses with her water to blood spiel and lazerz, which didn't particularly impress me either.

I dunno. Maybe I'm the dude looking at Mona Lisa going "ehh, it's almost as good as a photograph, but not quite there", but still, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that puts a damper on that theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a rewrite of an old article he wrote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, 2682 was already taken by Faminepulse, so that option would be ruled out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Na, when Djoric goes after a Series I article, he's more direct about it. If he wanted to do "682, but better," he'd probably name his article "2682" so everyone knows what's up.
That's what he did with 2166, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually thought it was an ersatz-682 remake thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna have to reread from a desktop and do some research, but my initial impression is that this is an impressive story that references things I'm not well-read enough to understand. I'll update this post soonish.

Edit: It's a chupacabra. I'm from New Mexico. How the fuck didn't I get that? Derp.
Also, you got Kalinin to upvote. Djoric, you're a wizard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I said in chat, I really love this one, and it's extremely well-written. Good job, Djoric.
E: how do you say something in cat

item.title: Chupassiah 2: Ecclesiastic Boogaloo
item.content:encoded:
I liked this article. I'd already pointed out some areas you needed to edit in chat, so I don't really have much to say here but my happiness that this article reached a form that has a shot at sticking.

Plus I couldn't resist the title pun and had to share it with the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude. Holy fuck. This is awesome. It could use some trimming, and the part about "wings made of light" seems rather un-clinical, but I upvoted happily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Freude, schöner Götterfunken
Tochter aus Elysium,
Wir betreten feuertrunken,
Himmlische, dein Heiligtum!
Deine Zauber binden wieder
Was die Mode streng geteilt;
Alle Menschen werden Brüder,
Wo dein sanfter Flügel weilt!

After a year, I finally did it.

We chupassiah now.
